sections.heading: Company Overview
sections.paragraph: GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100 trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster. The company embraces AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab's high-performance culture is driven by its values and continuous knowledge exchange, with team members collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: As a Professional Services Technical Architect at GitLab, you'll lead the technical direction of customer engagements from early scoping and discovery through delivery. You'll design high-level architectures and implementation plans for GitLab infrastructure and functionality, ensuring deliverables align with customer requirements and the Statement of Work (SOW). You'll coordinate and guide implementation work across GitLab Professional Services and partner consultants, support customers deploying GitLab in cloud and on-premises environments using tools like Terraform, Ansible, and the GitLab Environment Toolkit, and perform migrations to GitLab using Congregate. You'll provide DevOps and DevSecOps consulting and best practices, contribute reusable collateral such as documentation, delivery kits, and training materials, and share product release updates to help the team deliver consistent outcomes. This role combines deep technical expertise with customer-facing leadership in GitLab's remote, asynchronous, and values-driven environment.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Design and deliver GitLab reference architecture implementations for both self-managed and cloud environments, using infrastructure as code practices.
Lead source code management and CI/CD migrations to GitLab, including large-scale enterprise moves using Congregate.
Build repeatable delivery kits, documentation, and enablement materials that help Professional Services and partners deploy and adopt GitLab best practices.
Lead the full technical delivery lifecycle for GitLab Professional Services engagements, from early scoping and technical discovery through implementation and handoff.
Produce high-level and detailed technical designs for GitLab infrastructure and functionality, ensuring deliverables align with customer requirements and expectations.
Evaluate and communicate scalability and security considerations (including compliance constraints) in proposed GitLab reference architectures and implementation plans.

Deploy and configure GitLab in customer environments, including on-premises and major cloud providers, using Terraform, Ansible, and the GitLab Environment Toolkit (GET) aligned to reference architectures.
Plan and execute source system migrations to GitLab using Congregate, partnering closely with customer stakeholders to reduce risk, protect data integrity, and minimize downtime.
Provide DevOps and DevSecOps consulting and best-practice guidance, including advising on internal frameworks such as the Delivery Governance Framework (DGF) and GitLab Flow.

Coordinate and oversee implementation work across GitLab team members, partners, and customer points of contact (POCs), ensuring clear asynchronous communication, decision capture, effective execution, and high-quality outcomes.

Mentor Professional Services and partner consultants by contributing documentation, delivery kits, and training materials, and by leading enablement sessions on how to deliver and position service offerings.
Maintain and improve delivery automation assets with an emphasis on code cleanliness, maintainability, and appropriate unit/integration testing.

Support scoping and Statement of Work (SOW) creation with Professional Services Engagement Managers, stay current on monthly GitLab releases, and help Regional Delivery Managers with technical vetting and staffing assessments while maintaining a 55% billable utilization.
Review and provide input to Professional Services training materials and presentations.

sections.heading: Who We Are
sections.paragraph: Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations Cloud, a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. Samsara helps improve the safety, efficiency and sustainability of physical operations representing more than 40% of global GDP, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing. The team is shaping product solutions including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring, as part of a recently public company.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: Samsara's Strategic Customer Success team advises and guides its largest customers, ensuring they adopt Samsara and continuously gain business value from its products, serving as the trusted point of contact throughout the customer lifecycle: onboarding, adoption, advocacy, and renewal. This role is part of the Implementation team, responsible for helping Enterprise customers deploy and adopt Samsara's IoT solutions for their fleet and industrial assets. Picking up where the sales team leaves off, you will own the customer relationship and ensure a successful deployment of Samsara's solution. Customers span the entire range of businesses that power the economy, including trucking and transportation, food and beverage, passenger transit, utilities, and school buses, among others. The role is cross-functional, working alongside and connecting Sales, Support, Sales Engineering and Product. It requires exceptional customer-facing communication skills, the ability to quickly understand a customer's business, and comfort explaining technology products to both executives and day-to-day users. You will also champion, role model, and embed Samsara's cultural principles (Focus on Customer Success, Build for the Long Term, Adopt a Growth Mindset, Be Inclusive, Win as a Team) as the company scales globally.

sections.heading: Location & Travel
sections.paragraph: This is a remote position open to candidates residing in the US except Alaska, Austin Metro, Boulder Metro, California, Chicago Metro, Connecticut, Dallas Metro, Denver Metro, Houston Metro, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Rhode Island, Seattle Metro, and Washington, D.C. It may require travel to customer sites up to 10% of the time, including overnight stays.

You are the architect of your own career: this role won't be your last at Samsara, and you will have autonomy and opportunities to master your craft in a hyper-growth environment.
You want to be with the best: you will be surrounded by top professionals who win together, celebrate together, and support each other.

The Okta service integrates directly with an organization's existing directories and identity systems, as well as 6,500+ applications. sections.heading: Who We Are

Roboflow simplifies building and using computer vision models; today, over 1M+ developers, including those from half the Fortune 100, use Roboflow's machine learning open source and hosted tools for use cases including counting cells to accelerate cancer research, improving construction site safety, digitizing floor plans, preserving coral reef populations, and guiding drone flight. The role is responsible for project management, deployment, and adoption of Roboflow's computer vision solutions within customers' products, working closely with customers, engineering teams, and product managers, and combines expertise in computer vision, machine learning, and Python programming to design, implement, and optimize solutions.

Ibotta is looking for a highly motivated data engineer who cares about the craft of clean, well-governed data and understands why it matters more than ever in an AI-driven environment. You'll work on the data contract and metadata standards that underpin everything the squad builds, the compliance tooling that keeps Ibotta's data estate clean and auditable, and the pipelines that feed the AI readiness scoring system. AI Governance and Compliance: Support the discovery and classification of sensitive data across the data estate; maintain tagging and lineage automation frameworks; help translate data access and privacy policies into practical guardrails in partnership with the platform team; monitor AI usage across the company, defining new avenues for AI assistance.

AI Trust and Reliability: Build the systems that make people trust what the AI tells them about the data, such as query accuracy checks, guardrails that keep AI tools on certified data, and clear attribution so users can see where an answer came from; ensure monitoring is in place to catch problems before they erode confidence.

Semantic Layer Contributions: Build and maintain the semantic framework that makes data assets more legible to both people and AI systems, adding descriptions, context, and query guidance that improve the accuracy of natural language queries; work with analysts and domain experts to capture and formalize business definitions.
AI-Augmented Development: Proactively leverage AI tools (e.g., Claude, Databricks Genie) to accelerate development, maintain code quality, and explore new approaches to data engineering problems.
Cross-team Collaboration: Partner with adjacent teams to onboard data assets into the AI-ready platform, document processes, and contribute to the team's shared knowledge base.

Data Governance: Understanding of data governance concepts (metadata standards, data ownership, schema enforcement, access control) and experience keeping data assets clean and well-documented.
Data Quality Fundamentals: Experience with schema validation, data freshness monitoring, data observability tooling, or similar quality practices.

sections.heading: About Anthropic
sections.paragraph: Anthropic's mission is to create reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. We want AI to be safe and beneficial for our users and for society as a whole. sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: As a Technical Evangelist on the Startups team, you'll be the face of Anthropic in the startup ecosystem, driving awareness and activation among founders and their technical teams by showing them exactly what's possible with Claude and how to build it. Your focus is the startup developer community: getting builders from first API call to production, and turning early curiosity into committed platform adoption. This role sits at the intersection of technical depth and ecosystem development. Unlike a traditional DevRel or community role, this is built for someone who has shipped products, lived the early-stage grind, and can speak to a technical co-founder as a peer — someone who builds live demos and writes real code, not just talks about what Claude can do. Your primary mandate is inspiring adoption across the startup funnel, turning ecosystem touchpoints into active, building developers on Anthropic's platform. You'll work closely with the Startups GTM team to convert relationships with VCs, accelerators, and founder communities into developers who are genuinely building.

sections.heading: Driving Developer Adoption

Develop a repeatable playbook for turning ecosystem touchpoints into measurable developer sign-ups and active usage
Partner closely with the Startups GTM team to create clean handoffs from relationship to activation to retention

Build live demos and prototypes that showcase Claude's real capabilities for common startup use cases, not marketing-grade toy examples
Partner with the Product team to stay current on platform capabilities and ensure content reflects what's actually differentiated about Claude

Collaborate with the Startups Partnerships team to ensure VC and accelerator relationships translate into real developer engagement and usage metrics
Stay closely aligned with Product on platform strategy so your content and programs reflect what Claude can genuinely do

Built for pioneers by pioneers, CoreWeave delivers a platform of technology, tools, and teams that enables innovators to build and scale AI with confidence, trusted by leading AI labs, startups, and global enterprises. Founded in 2017, CoreWeave became a publicly traded company (Nasdaq: CRWV) in March 2025.

sections.heading: About The Role

sections.heading: About NVIDIA
sections.paragraph: NVIDIA is redefining computing at the intersection of graphics, accelerated computing, and AI, building platforms that make advanced AI useful wherever people create, work, and build.

sections.heading: About The Role

You will be a trusted partner to external developers and an internal advocate for their needs, helping partners adopt and optimize NVIDIA technologies, remove integration barriers, and deliver excellent on-premise and combined AI experiences. The ideal candidate combines hands-on AI software expertise, strong partner instincts, and the ability to transform emerging ecosystem opportunities into repeatable technical and go-to-market outcomes.

sections.heading: What You'll Be Doing
bullets: Own a portfolio of strategic local AI partners as NVIDIA's primary relationship and technical engagement lead, from opportunity identification and onboarding through integration, launch, and ongoing adoption
Serve as a trusted technical advisor and problem solver for model developers, AI application and tool providers, agent-platform companies, inference-runtime teams, and open-source communities
Drive adoption and optimization of NVIDIA accelerated computing, AI runtimes, SDKs, and developer tools across local and hybrid AI workflows on Windows and Linux
Define joint partner plans with clear technical milestones, success criteria, launch readiness, and measurable ecosystem outcomes

Engage directly with partner engineers, technical leaders, founders, and development groups to understand goals, unblock integrations, and promote guidelines for performance, reliability, and user experience
Represent the voice of the partner inside NVIDIA, translating field insights into actionable product requirements and roadmap feedback
Collaborate with engineering, product management, Developer Technology, business development, marketing, legal, and sales to structure and complete complex partner engagements
Create and scale technical enablement through reference integrations, samples, tutorials, workshops, playbooks, and partner-ready launch materials
Represent NVIDIA at developer events, conferences, community forums, and partner briefings
Track emerging models, frameworks, runtimes, developer behaviors, and competitive shifts, and turn those signals into partner priorities and ecosystem strategy

sections.heading: What We Need To See

8+ years of professional experience in software engineering, developer relations, developer advocacy, solutions architecture, technical partnerships, or a similar technology position
Strong hands-on experience with AI/ML software, including generative AI models, inference, model serving, or AI application development

Experience building and leading technical relationships with software companies, independent software vendors, startups, or open-source projects from initial engagement through production delivery

Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills, able to explain complex technical concepts to audiences ranging from hands-on developers to senior executives
Strong judgment, initiative, and follow-through in fast-moving environments with incomplete information and multiple stakeholders

sections.heading: Ways To Stand Out

Experience with LLMs, VLMs, diffusion models, speech models, retrieval systems, tool use, or agentic application patterns
Depth in one or more performance-critical areas such as quantization, distributed or multi-GPU optimization

sections.heading: About Rockstar Games
sections.paragraph: At Rockstar Games, we create world-class entertainment experiences, working on some of the most rewarding, large-scale creative projects in any entertainment medium within an inclusive, highly-motivated environment.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: Rockstar is seeking a talented Senior Developer Advocate to support its Creator Platform team, which includes the FiveM and RedM multiplayer modification platforms. The role focuses on improving the developer experience for creators, including translating complex technical concepts into clear, accessible guides, tutorials, and API references to empower a worldwide community of creators and server owners. This is a full-time, in-office position based out of Rockstar's NYC headquarters in Downtown Manhattan.

sections.heading: What The Team Does
sections.paragraph: The Creator Platform Team delivers a technology platform that lets players experience community-created content on fully customized dedicated servers, where creators develop their own game modes and modifications in a variety of scripting languages. The team also runs the Cfx Marketplace, a curated digital storefront where GTAV and RDR2 creators can share and sell custom scripts and assets, and produces developer documentation, tutorials, and learning resources for the creator community.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
bullets: Evangelize the use of Creator Platform tools and technology to ensure new and existing creators have a clear path to success on FiveM and RedM
Advocate for ongoing improvement of the developer experience through prioritized feedback from the creator community and Developer Relations
Support the development of developer-oriented programs to grow the next generation of creators on the Platforms
Drive the maturity of the Developer Experience, including comprehensive developer documentation, getting-started guides, tutorials, how-to articles, and API references for FiveM and RedM
Create step-by-step guides and templates that teach developers and creators how to build scripts, game modes, maps, props, and other content

Collaborate closely with Creator Platform engineering teams to understand new features, platform changes, and technical details, and translate them into creator-facing changelogs, release notes, and migration guides
Review and improve existing developer documentation for clarity, accuracy, and completeness as the Creator Platforms evolve

sections.heading: Requirements
bullets: 6+ years of experience in technical writing, developer documentation, or a related role, ideally in gaming or software development
Demonstrated ability to explain complex technical concepts in clear, concise, well-structured written content
Familiarity with at least one scripting or programming language (e.g., Lua, JavaScript/TypeScript, C#, or similar)
Experience working with documentation tooling and platforms (e.g., Markdown, static site generators, wikis, or docs-as-code workflows)
Strong technical comprehension, with the ability to quickly learn new technologies, APIs, and developer tools
Excellent writing and editing skills, with experience producing tutorials, API references, or developer guides
Ability to collaborate effectively with Engineering, Product Management, Developer Relations, and Community teams
Comfort working with version control systems (e.g., Git) and contributing to documentation repositories
A user-focused mindset when producing content

sections.heading: Nice To Have
bullets: Previous experience with the FiveM/RedM ecosystem, Grand Theft Auto modding, or similar game modding communities
Experience writing public-facing documentation for game engines, modding frameworks, or other user-facing technologies
Experience producing video tutorials, interactive code samples, or other multimedia learning content
Understanding of creator workflows and asset pipelines in UGC or modding ecosystems
Experience contributing to or collaborating with open source projects and developer communities

sections.heading: How To Apply
sections.paragraph: Apply with a resume and cover letter demonstrating how you meet the skills above. If Rockstar wants to move forward with your application, a recruiter will reach out to explain next steps and guide you through the process.

sections.heading: About ClickHouse
sections.paragraph: Recognized on the 2025 Forbes Cloud 100 list, ClickHouse is one of the most innovative and fast-growing private cloud companies. With more than 3,000 customers and ARR that has grown over 250 percent year over year, ClickHouse leads the market in real-time analytics, data warehousing, observability, and AI workloads. The company's sustained, accelerating momentum was recently validated by a $400M Series D financing round. Customers include Capital One, Lovable, Decagon, Polymarket, Airwallex, Meta, Cursor, Sony, and Tesla.

sections.heading: About the Role

This role is remote, but candidates must live near a major airport due to frequent travel (~50%+) to support meetups, conferences, and customer/partner events.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Lead and grow ClickHouse's AMER meetup network, coordinating events, speakers, logistics, and community engagement.
Serve as the primary connector for developer-focused initiatives across engineering, product, marketing, and sales.
Act as a liaison between ClickHouse, the community, partners, and customers to identify opportunities and gather feedback.
Enable internal teams to create technical content, case studies, or demos via subject matter experts and community insights.
Track and report metrics measuring the success and impact of developer engagement, meetups, and advocacy programs.
Represent ClickHouse at conferences, webinars, meetups, and other technical forums.
Support engineers in online communities such as Discord, Twitter, Slack, and GitHub Discussions.

sections.heading: What You Bring
bullets: Strong technical background in data systems, analytics, distributed systems, or related domains.
Experience engaging and enabling developer communities, including running meetups, events, or open source programs.
Ability to coordinate across multiple teams (engineering, product, marketing, sales) and with external partners.
Excellent communication skills — able to translate complex technical topics for diverse audiences.
Strategic thinking and the ability to drive programs that scale across multiple geographies and stakeholders.
Comfortable being a visible, public advocate for ClickHouse and its community.
Willingness and ability to travel 50%+ of the time; remote role requires living near a major airport.

sections.heading: About Figma
sections.paragraph: Figma is growing its team of passionate creatives and builders on a mission to make design accessible to all. Figma's platform helps teams bring ideas to life — whether brainstorming, creating a prototype, translating designs into code, or iterating with AI — empowering teams to streamline workflows, move faster, and work together in real time from anywhere in the world.

sections.heading: About The Role

This advocate will support content creation and community building through field events and livestreams, go-to-market motions, product initiatives, and 1:1 customer engagements with the sales team. Advocates at Figma are practitioners first — people who understand the complexity of modern product development firsthand and can advocate for user needs internally. This hands-on role blends technical depth, communication, and strong product intuition: you'll write production-quality code, build demos and tools, and directly support developers and technical teams, while also operating as a connective layer that translates between customer needs, product decisions, and go-to-market execution. This is a full-time role in the Tokyo office, within a hybrid environment.

sections.heading: What You'll Do

Own regional technical presence: play a leading role across JAPAC field events, activations, and key moments, representing Figma to developer audiences
Build real things: create production-quality demos, prototypes, and tools that reflect how modern teams actually build, not just toy examples
Create scalable technical content: author guides, code samples, and technical narratives that help developers adopt Figma in real workflows
Influence product direction: bring structured, high-signal feedback from customers and the community into Product, helping shape features before and after launch
Bridge design and development: help teams operationalize workflows across design systems, tokens, and developer tooling

Background in growth SaaS business

sections.heading: About Roku
sections.paragraph: Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, with a mission to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. Roku connects consumers to the content they love, enables content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provides advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers. It is a fast-growing public company offering the opportunity to reach millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining experience across a variety of disciplines.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: This role is on the Roku OS Streaming Media team, responsible for Roku's Media engine, implementing new features and designing algorithms that deliver flawless video playback to all users. This includes optimizing network interactions between players and CDN, implementing adaptive streaming stacks, integrating DRM systems, and developing tools to monitor performance. The features and performance improvements implemented in this role directly impact millions of users every day.

sections.heading: What You'll Be Doing
bullets: Extend Roku's player feature set to support new business opportunities and the latest multimedia specifications
Continuously improve playback on Roku devices, delivering a world-class player experience under all operating conditions
Work independently and in small groups to deliver new features and functionality on time
Demonstrate excellence in C++ & Python development and deliver high-performance, ultra-reliable software
Make extensive changes to an existing C++ code base that are modular and easy to maintain
Dig deep to find solutions to tough problems affecting users

Experience in API design and developing portable code
Ability to accurately estimate software tasks and work to schedule

sections.heading: About Aurora
sections.paragraph: Aurora's mission is to deliver the benefits of self-driving technology safely, quickly, and broadly. The Aurora Driver aims to create a new era in mobility and logistics, bringing a safer, more efficient, and more accessible future to everyone. Aurora hires talented people with diverse backgrounds to help build a transportation ecosystem that makes roads safer, gets crucial goods where they need to go, and makes mobility more efficient and accessible.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: Aurora is seeking a seasoned Staff Embedded Linux Developer to architect, design, and implement system-level software for its next-generation embedded platforms. This high-impact role requires deep expertise in Linux kernel internals, BSP customization with the Yocto Project, and hands-on experience with hardware interfaces like PCIe, BIOS, and DDR RAM. The ideal candidate will lead complex debugging efforts, collaborate closely with hardware teams during board bring-up, and drive performance optimization across all system components.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Lead the design, development, customization, and maintenance of Board Support Packages (BSPs) for various hardware platforms using the Yocto Project build system
Configure, build, and optimize the Linux kernel, bootloaders (U-Boot), and root file systems for custom hardware, ensuring system stability and performance
Develop, port, and debug Linux device drivers for hardware interfaces including PCIe, DDR, Ethernet, I2C, SPI, and USB
Collaborate with hardware engineers during initial board bring-up, debugging issues at the register and schematic levels using tools such as JTAG, oscilloscopes, and logic analyzers
Implement and enhance functionality related to the PCIe subsystem, including DMA, memory management, and interrupt handling
Work with BIOS/UEFI configurations and integrate them with the embedded Linux environment as required for specific platforms (e.g., x86 architectures)
Optimize system performance, boot time, memory footprint, and power consumption for embedded devices
Develop and maintain CI/CD pipelines and automated testing workflows for the embedded software stack
Create requirements and design documentation, conduct code reviews, mentor junior engineers, and develop unit test plans

sections.heading: Required Qualifications
bullets: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field
Minimum of 8+ years of hands-on experience in embedded Linux development, with a strong background in system-level programming and board bring-up
Deep expertise in the Yocto Project, including custom layer development, recipes (BitBake), and image generation
Strong proficiency in C/C++ programming for embedded and kernel-level development
In-depth knowledge of Linux kernel internals, memory management, scheduling, and device driver architecture
Hands-on experience with debugging tools like GDB, JTAG, Lauterbach, and oscilloscopes
Strong problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams

Exposure to open-source contributions and community collaboration practices
Knowledge of networking protocols (TCP/IP, UDP) and real-time operating system (RTOS) concepts
Knowledge of the Telecommunication and/or GNSS domain

sections.heading: Company Overview
sections.paragraph: Our mission is to automate coding. The first step is building the best tool for professional programmers, using a combination of inventive research, design, and engineering. The organization is very flat, and the team is small and talent dense, favoring people who are truth-seeking, passionate, and creative, and who enjoy spirited debate, crazy ideas, and shipping code.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: You will lead the Core Services team at Cursor, owning the critical shared services that sit between the product surfaces and the infrastructure layer: auth, webhooks, and the backend systems that power agent workflows. You'll build and operate the systems that every product team depends on, ensuring they are reliable, well-abstracted, and ready for whatever comes next. You'll set technical direction for authentication, SCM integrations, webhook infrastructure, and end-to-end agent reliability, and you'll lead a small, high-impact team, write and review code, and partner closely with product and infrastructure teams to ensure Cursor's backend is rock-solid.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Own authentication: design and implement a scalable auth architecture that secures one of the most widely-used developer tools in the world.
Power the agent backend: own the infrastructure behind Cursor's agent workflows, optimizing for end-to-end performance and reliability.
Build a robust webhook server: handle high-throughput event delivery with proper retry semantics, dead-letter queues, and observability.
Harden SCM integrations: build resilient abstractions over source control providers.
Drive end-to-end agent reliability: partner with the client and inference teams to improve the full path from user action to agent response.
Hire and grow the team: source, interview, and close top backend and services talent, and develop engineers through coaching, mentorship, and high-leverage project assignments.

sections.heading: You May Be A Fit If
bullets: You have led engineering teams building and operating critical backend services, especially auth systems, API gateways, or webhook infrastructure.

Collaborate with AI Engineering teams and other internal AI initiatives, building on shared infrastructure and investing in security-specific tooling.

Technically credible, able to review detection logic, code (e.g., Python), and the architecture of security systems (AI-driven or otherwise).
Experience building automation systems for security operations workflows: detection platforms, AI-driven detection capabilities, case triage, investigation, response automation, or analyst tooling.

sections.heading: Company Overview
sections.paragraph: LangChain's mission is to make intelligent agents ubiquitous, building the foundation for agent engineering and helping developers move from prototypes to production-ready AI agents. The company has raised $125M at Series B from IVP, Sequoia, Benchmark, CapitalG, and Sapphire Ventures. Its platform includes LangSmith (Observability, Evaluation, Deployment, Fleet, and Sandboxes), open source frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, and Deep Agents), and the newly launched LangSmith Engine for autonomous agent improvement. LangChain has 100M+ monthly open source downloads, 6,000+ active LangSmith customers, and 5 of the Fortune 10 use LangSmith in production (plus 35% of the Fortune 500 overall), including teams at Klarna, Clay, Coinbase, Workday, Lyft, Cloudflare, Harvey, Rippling, Vanta, LinkedIn, Monday.com, Nvidia, and Bridgewater.

sections.heading: About The Team
sections.paragraph: SmithDB is LangChain's internal database team, building a storage and query layer purpose-built for AI observability and evaluation. Within six months the team went from idea to a production system offering industry-leading performance and scalability for agent observability data. It's a small, fast team of systems engineers tackling storage layout, query execution, compaction, and scaling toward trillions of agent traces. The team develops in Rust, runs on Kubernetes, and integrates tightly with S3/GCS/Azure Blob, on a greenfield system with real production load and no legacy constraints.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: LangChain is looking for a hands-on Engineering Manager to lead the SmithDB team — not a pure people-management role. You'll write production code, review PRs at the systems level, make architectural calls, and be in the weeds alongside your engineers, while also owning team health, hiring, technical roadmap, and coordination with the broader LangSmith platform. The ideal candidate has deep systems or database engineering experience and genuinely prefers to stay technical while growing a team.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Write and review production Rust code across ingestion, query execution, and storage layers.
Lead architectural decisions on storage format, compaction, indexing, and query planning.
Drive performance investigations using memory and CPU profiling tools; own the path from profiling to shipped fix.
Design and harden the distributed deployment of SmithDB services on Kubernetes (multi-tenant, high-throughput, low-latency).
Contribute to cloud object store integrations (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) and set the standard for how SmithDB manages data at rest and in flight.
Build and maintain observability for the engine itself: metrics, tracing, debug tooling.
Manage a small but growing team of systems engineers: set goals, run 1:1s, provide technical mentorship, and grow careers.
Own the SmithDB technical roadmap in partnership with LangSmith product and engineering leadership.
Communicate progress, risks, and tradeoffs clearly to the broader organization with concise, decision-ready updates.

sections.heading: What You'll Bring
bullets: 7+ years in systems or database engineering, with at least 2 years in a technical lead or engineering management role.
Production Rust experience — you can write it, review it, and have opinions on how to structure it at scale.
Deep understanding of database or storage engine internals: query execution, storage layouts, indexing, compaction.
Proficiency in systems performance analysis: memory allocators, CPU hotspots, lock contention, async runtimes (Tokio).
Experience deploying and operating distributed services on Kubernetes in a production, multi-tenant environment.
Familiarity with cloud object storage (S3-compatible APIs, consistency models, cost/performance tradeoffs).
Proven track record of managing engineers — able to recruit, retain, and level-up a team without losing technical edge.

sections.heading: Nice To Have
bullets: Experience building or contributing to columnar storage formats (Parquet, Arrow), OLAP query engines, or time-series stores.
Background in observability infrastructure or tracing pipelines (OpenTelemetry, ClickHouse, Prometheus).
Experience scaling a system from early-stage to hundreds of billions / trillions of records.

sections.heading: Compensation
sections.paragraph: Salary range: $215,000-$260,000 USD. Compensation includes base salary, variable compensation for relevant roles, meaningful equity, benefits, and perks; actual compensation and offerings vary based on role and level.

sections.heading: Company Overview
sections.paragraph: Palantir builds the world's leading software for data-driven decisions and operations. By bringing the right data to the people who need it, our platforms empower our partners to develop lifesaving drugs, forecast supply chain disruptions, locate missing children, and more.

sections.heading: Role Overview
sections.paragraph: Forward Deployed Software Engineers (FDSEs) work directly with customers to quickly understand their greatest problems and design and implement solutions to use data against them. Projects often start with an open-ended question like "Why are we delaying so many flights?" or "How can we better identify instances of money laundering?" As an FDSE, you'll apply your problem-solving ability, creativity, and technical skills to help organizations use their data to drive real impact, gaining rare insight into some of the world's most important industries and institutions. Your responsibilities look similar to those of a startup CTO: you'll work in small teams with minimal supervision and own end-to-end execution of high-stakes projects, spanning architecture discussions, wrangling massive-scale data, coding custom web apps, speaking with customer executives, and setting team strategy. You'll work autonomously and make decisions independently, within a community that supports and challenges you as you grow.

sections.heading: Our Principles
bullets: Impact: We take on meaningful and challenging projects that change the world for the better.
Ownership: We see projects through from beginning to end, working through any obstacles we encounter.
Collaboration: We work internally with people from a variety of backgrounds (other FDSEs, product teams, Deployment Strategists) and externally with customers, often on site.
Trust: We trust each other to effectively manage time and priorities and give people the space to think for themselves.
Growth: We encourage ourselves and our peers to seek new challenges and find new ways to innovate and share knowledge.
Learning: We often face entirely novel problems where we need to pick up new knowledge and learn how to use it to make progress.

sections.heading: What We Value
bullets: Strong engineering background, preferred in fields such as Computer Science, Mathematics, Software Engineering, or Physics.
Experience with logistics, materiel, sustainment, aviation, or readiness analysis is a plus.
Familiarity with data structures, storage systems, cloud infrastructure, front-end frameworks, and other technical tools.
Understanding of how technical decisions impact the user of what you're building.
Strong coder with demonstrated proficiency in Python, Java, C++, TypeScript/JavaScript, or similar.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical individuals in a rapidly changing environment with dynamic objectives.
Demonstrated ability to continuously learn, work independently, and make decisions with minimal supervision.
Willingness and interest to travel as needed.

sections.heading: Requirements
sections.paragraph: Active US Security Clearance at or above the Top Secret level, or willingness to obtain/upgrade to Top Secret.

sections.heading: Compensation & Benefits
sections.paragraph: The estimated salary range for this position is $135,000-$200,000/year. Total compensation may also include restricted stock units, a sign-on bonus, and other potential future incentives; this estimate excludes the value of any sign-on bonus, benefits, or long-term incentives, and actual compensation depends on each individual's relevant qualifications, work experience, skills, and other factors. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision insurance (with eligible dependents), voluntary life insurance, employer-paid basic life, AD&D, and disability insurance, and commuter benefits, among others.

sections.heading: About Cloudflare
sections.paragraph: Cloudflare is on a mission to help build a better Internet. The company runs one of the world's largest networks, powering millions of websites and Internet properties for customers ranging from individual bloggers to SMBs to Fortune 500 companies, protecting and accelerating any Internet application without adding hardware, installing software, or changing a line of code. Cloudflare was named to Entrepreneur Magazine's Top Company Cultures list and ranked among Fast Company's World's Most Innovative Companies. The company looks for builders who spot the cracks in the Internet everyone else has learned to live with, and values AI-native curiosity, iteration, and leveraging AI to ship faster.

sections.heading: Role Summary
sections.paragraph: Locations: Austin/TX, Dallas/TX, or Atlanta/GA. As a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE), you will be a dedicated Cloudflare expert embedded within the team of one of Cloudflare's most strategic and fast-paced customers, serving as the primary technical resource, architecting complex multi-stage migrations, and providing hands-on configuration for unique web infrastructure requirements. Cloudflare is looking for a proactive, high-trust partner who thrives in agile environments, bridges to internal product teams, and has a deep passion for the future of AI-driven security.

bullets: Customer Engagement & Technical Leadership: Serve as a trusted advisor to key enterprise customers, providing technical insights, best practices, and strategic recommendations — particularly on Cloudflare's Workers and Workers AI platforms.
Lead architectural discussions, design sessions, and solution reviews to ensure optimal application of Cloudflare's offerings in customer environments.
Diagnose complex technical issues and deliver effective, strategic solutions in collaboration with support and product teams.
Architect Application Security: Design and configure tailored solutions focused on Application Security, using Cloudflare Workers to optimize and direct global traffic, and Network Security.
Master Infrastructure as Code: Support the account team by navigating and managing complex configurations heavily reliant on Terraform.

Act as a Strategic Liaison: Serve as a high-speed conduit between the customer and internal Cloudflare teams (Product, Support, Engineering), advocating for customer needs and influencing product roadmaps.
Navigate Modern Communication: Manage complex, fast-moving technical discussions primarily through Slack, guiding the customer even when formal calls are hard to schedule.
Drive AI Innovation: Leverage deep knowledge of AI/LLM concepts to align Cloudflare's capabilities with the customer's AI-focused culture and future technological goals.
Provide Long-Term Guidance: Stay with the account post-migration as a trusted advisor identifying future strategic initiatives, including a transition into Network Security and SASE architectures.

sections.heading: Must-Have Skills

Extensive, proven knowledge of Cloudflare's Application Security, Workers and Workers AI, cybersecurity, web performance, and cloud infrastructure.
Extensive experience implementing and supporting complex technical solutions using Terraform for infrastructure management.
Demonstrated ability to build trust and rapport in challenging environments, remaining flexible and persistent in establishing regular cadences with stakeholders.
Exceptional ability to navigate "Slack-first" communication cultures, providing clear guidance and managing technical threads under pressure.
Strong technical understanding of, and passion for, AI/LLM concepts (listing cut off in source).

sections.heading: Team Overview

It's a small, senior, field-first pod — part startup, part consultancy — defining the playbook as it goes. As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you are Instacart's hands-on technical presence inside enterprise customer environments, sitting inside retailers' and partners' ecosystems, understanding their infrastructure, and building agentic AI solutions on top of it. You'll collaborate with partner technical leaders on solution designs and work closely with Instacart R&D, informing platform evolution and serving as the bridge between what's built internally and what actually works in the real world.

sections.heading: About the Job
bullets: Embed with enterprise retail customers and partners to understand their technical environments, data systems, and business workflows.
Design, build, and deploy AI and agentic solutions tailored to each customer's specific infrastructure and needs, across Instacart's full platform suite.
Extend, adapt, and integrate Instacart's core AI capabilities to fit customer ecosystems, even when those systems are undocumented or non-standard.

sections.heading: Who We Are
sections.paragraph: Roboflow's mission is to make the world programmable with artificial intelligence, building the tools, community, and resources to get there. Roboflow has raised over $63 million from Y Combinator, Google Ventures, Craft Ventures, Sam Altman, Lachy Groom, and other leading software investors.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
sections.paragraph: Roboflow is hiring Forward Deployed Engineers (FDEs) to be the first technical hands on new customers' Roboflow deployments. After the Sales and Solutions Architecture team closes a deal and validates the technical approach, the FDE takes it from proof-of-concept to production — the critical 0-to-1 phase. You'll embed directly with new customers, working alongside their engineering and operations teams to build and deploy their first production computer vision workflows, configuring edge devices and data pipelines so models perform in real-world conditions rather than just in a demo. You'll also serve as Roboflow's eyes and ears in the field, feeding insights on the gap between what customers think they want and what they actually need back to Product and Engineering. Once the deployment works in production and the customer's team is equipped to operate it, you hand off to Roboflow's Implementation Engineers, who scale, optimize, and expand the solution.

sections.heading: Role and Responsibilities
bullets: 0-to-1 Deployment: Take validated proof-of-concepts from the pre-sales process and build the first production deployment, including data pipeline setup, model optimization, edge device configuration, and integration with customer infrastructure.
Edge-First Engineering: Deploy and operate computer vision systems on edge hardware in physical environments with unpredictable conditions and unreliable connectivity.

Embed with Customers: Work on-site or deeply embedded with the customer's engineering team during the initial deployment phase (typically 4-12 weeks per engagement), building trust and transferring knowledge.

Production Engineering: Write production-grade code that will live in the customer's environment, handling lighting variability, camera calibration, model drift, network latency, and edge hardware constraints.
Be Our Eyes and Ears in the Field: Surface the gap between what customers say they want, what they actually need, and what machine operators think, feeding that back to Product and Engineering.

Knowledge Transfer & Handoff: Document deployment architecture, create runbooks, train the customer's team to operate the system independently, and provide a clean handoff to Implementation Engineers.
De-risk New Deployments: Identify and resolve technical risks early, finding alternatives before a customer's environment becomes a project failure.
Shape the Deployment Playbook: Codify repeatable deployment patterns, starter templates, and reusable artifacts that make future deployments faster and more reliable.

sections.heading: About Anthropic
sections.paragraph: Anthropic's mission is to create reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. We want AI to be safe and beneficial for our users and for society as a whole. Our team is a quickly growing group of committed researchers, engineers, policy experts, and business leaders working together to build beneficial AI systems.

sections.heading: Team Overview
sections.paragraph: The Endpoint team (Client Platform Engineering) treats Anthropic's device fleet as a distributed platform, not a collection of laptops. They run their own MDM as a production service and manage every piece of device configuration as code — policies, configuration profiles, queries, remediation scripts, and software all ship through pull requests, CI, a staging environment, and a canary group before reaching the fleet. The fleet spans macOS, Windows, and a growing mobile footprint.

sections.heading: Role Overview
sections.paragraph: You'll own the platform end to end: the infrastructure underneath the MDM, the configuration on top of it, the patching and software pipelines that keep thousands of devices patched and secure, and the telemetry that tells us what is actually true on every device. You'll build zero touch provisioning that turns a sealed box into a productive machine on day one, manage rapid patching enforcement schedules while maintaining a good user experience, and build automation and Claude-driven workflows to eliminate operational toil. The role sits at the intersection of security and developer experience — working with Security teams on hardening, compliance controls, and detection and response, and with developer and infrastructure teams to keep controls from getting in the way of work. It also lays the groundwork for access decisions based on device trust. The team is deliberately lean and runs with high autonomy; you'll help define the endpoint roadmap, make architecture decisions, and own the platform every Anthropic employee's work runs on. Your work will directly shape how the company scales to AI Safety Level 4 and beyond.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
bullets: Own endpoint configuration as code: author, review, test, and progressively roll out MDM policies, configuration profiles, and remediation scripts across macOS, Windows, and mobile, with canary stages and rollback built in
Operate the MDM platform itself as a production service, including infrastructure as code, observability, upgrades, and incident response
Build patch management automation with rapid enforcement timelines while maintaining good user experience
Design zero touch provisioning that turns a sealed box into a productive machine on day one
Run software distribution for the fleet, including managed app distribution for mobile devices
Turn fleet telemetry into policy, dashboards, and early drift warnings, and build automation with Claude that removes operational toil
Partner with Corporate Security on endpoint hardening, binary authorization, and compliance controls
Serve as the deep escalation tier for endpoint issues IT Operations can't resolve

sections.heading: Qualifications
bullets: 8+ years building secure IT systems in complex environments, or (for Staff level) have led projects spanning multiple teams that changed how an organization operates
Have managed endpoint fleets of thousands of macOS and Windows devices through a modern MDM
Treat endpoint configuration as code and have moved past clicking in consoles, whether through scripted deployments or full GitOps

Go deep on one platform (macOS internals such as launchD, configuration profiles, TCC, and system extensions, or Windows internals such as CSPs, the registry, PowerShell, and BitLocker) and are genuinely hands-on with the other
Excel at solving ambiguous problems with multiple stakeholders
Communicate technical concepts clearly to any audience
View IT Engineering as requiring product engineering rigor
Successfully deliver complex projects from conception to production
Write clear documentation as a natural part of your workflow

sections.heading: About Anthropic
sections.paragraph: Anthropic's mission is to create reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. We want AI to be safe and beneficial for our users and for society as a whole. The team is a quickly growing group of committed researchers, engineers, policy experts, and business leaders working together to build beneficial AI systems.

sections.heading: About The Role
sections.paragraph: Anthropic's Application Security team secures the systems that build, serve, and increasingly are Claude. The attack surface is unlike most AppSec work: multi-agent orchestration, sandboxed code execution, agents holding delegated credentials, and untrusted tool output crossing trust boundaries — problems with little prior art and no off-the-shelf playbook. The team uses Claude as its primary tool across every part of the job: it drives static analysis, drafts and fixes vulnerabilities as pull requests, performs first-line bug bounty triage, and assists threat modeling for design reviews. The human work is the judgment layer — system-level reasoning, deciding what matters, and building the next thing the model can't do yet. This is a builder's role on a senior team; every engineer owns a system end-to-end, and the team's work has shaped customer-facing product security, including Claude Code's security review tooling, its security guidance plugin, sandboxing, and auto mode.

sections.heading: Key Responsibilities
bullets: Design, build, and operate Claude-powered security systems — LLM-driven code analysis, automated vulnerability remediation, AI-assisted threat modeling — and own one or more of them end-to-end, including the cross-functional relationships that come with it
Lead secure design reviews and threat modeling for novel AI systems, identifying risks that don't map to existing frameworks
Evolve a public bug bounty program where automation handles routine triage and root-cause work, and engineers handle escalations and corner cases
Partner with Product, Infrastructure, and Research teams as an embedded security owner — consulting on launches, shaping architecture, and influencing decisions where security is the constraint
Share an operational on-call rotation with the rest of the team — bounty escalations, incident response, and launch consults on systems serving Claude in production

sections.heading: Minimum Qualifications
bullets: Hands-on application and infrastructure security experience, including cloud and containerized environments
Production-quality coding ability in at least one of Python, Go, Rust, or TypeScript, with a track record of building durable systems rather than one-off scripts
Practical threat-modeling and vulnerability-identification skills — you've found and reasoned about real bugs in real systems, even if breaking isn't your primary mode
Demonstrated ability to operate with high autonomy and ambiguity
Clear technical communication with both engineers and leadership

sections.heading: Preferred Qualifications
bullets: 7+ years in application security, security engineering, or security-focused software engineering
Already use LLMs as a core part of how you work, with opinions about where they help and where they don't
Experience securing agentic, code-execution, or LLM-integrated systems specifically
Prior ownership of a bug bounty program, vulnerability disclosure program, or vulnerability-management infrastructure at scale
Background building security automation or developer-facing security tooling
Offensive security or penetration testing experience

sections.heading: Representative Projects
bullets: An increasingly autonomous vulnerability pipeline — LLM-driven code analysis finds the issue, scores it for real exploitability, and opens the fix PR, with humans as the review step rather than the author
Bug bounty operations where Claude handles first-line triage and drafting, and engineers focus on the reports that actually need judgment
AI-assisted threat modeling that generates intake questions, drafts the model, and recommends which design reviews need a human in the room
Automated dependency vulnerability remediation across Anthropic's codebase
The company-wide vulnerability dashboard and SLA enforcement layer every engineering team works against
Threat models and security architecture for agentic product surfaces — code execution sandboxing, agent identity and delegated auth, tool-use boundaries

sections.heading: About The Role

You lead AI architecture work in large projects and across teams, collaborating with Data Scientists, AI/ML Engineers, Cloud Architects, data specialists, and business stakeholders, and contribute to Solita's technical leadership in applied AI by shaping best practices, supporting pre-sales and solution planning, and mentoring colleagues.

sections.heading: About the Team

sections.heading: About the Role
sections.paragraph: This role operates the next generation of compute clusters that power OpenAI's frontier research, blending distributed systems engineering with hands-on infrastructure work on OpenAI's largest datacenters. You will scale Kubernetes clusters to massive scale, automate bare-metal bring-up, and build the software layer that hides the complexity of a large number of nodes across multiple data centers, working at the intersection of hardware and software where speed and reliability are critical.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Spin up and scale large Kubernetes clusters, including automation for provisioning, bootstrapping, and cluster lifecycle management.
Build software abstractions that unify multiple clusters and present a seamless interface to training workloads.

Improve operational metrics such as reducing cluster restart times (e.g., from hours to minutes) and accelerating firmware or OS upgrade cycles.
Integrate networking and hardware health systems to deliver end-to-end reliability across servers, switches, and data center infrastructure.
Develop monitoring and observability systems to detect issues early and keep clusters stable under extreme load.
Be expected to execute at the same level as a software engineer.

sections.heading: You Might Thrive If

You bring strong programming or scripting skills (Python, Go, or similar) and familiarity with Infrastructure-as-Code tools such as Terraform or CloudFormation.
You are comfortable with bare-metal Linux environments, GPU hardware, and large-scale networking.
You enjoy solving fast-moving, high-impact operational problems and building automation to eliminate manual work.
You can balance careful engineering with the urgency of keeping mission-critical systems running.

bullets: Experience as an infrastructure, systems, or distributed systems engineer in large-scale or high-availability environments.

sections.heading: About OpenAI
sections.paragraph: OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity, working to push the boundaries of AI system capabilities and safely deploy them through its products.

sections.heading: About Riot Games

sections.heading: About the Role

You'll be a champion of the live experience for players, writing code to make incidents easier to detect, faster to mitigate, and less likely to occur. You'll develop experience with client-service interaction to increase knowledge of how Riot's games are experienced by players, and grow your expertise with engineering best practices to ensure code quality and testing are top of the line.

sections.heading: Responsibilities

Lead the investigation and mitigation of live incidents as an Incident Commander, and participate in post-incident RCAs.
Use investigation and troubleshooting to return impacted systems to service quickly and keep games running for players.
Identify improvements to the team's tools, processes, and documentation, as well as broader improvements to prevent incidents and drive down incident impact for Riot's live services.
Write tooling, automation, and services to implement these improvements.
Write and understand code in the team's codebases, utilizing appropriate data structures, algorithms, and software testing best practices.

sections.heading: Work Schedule
sections.paragraph: The standard working schedule consists of four 10-hour days, including one weekend day and three days off per week, supporting a follow-the-sun operational model that ensures continuous global coverage across timezones.

sections.heading: Required Qualifications

2+ years of industry experience or an advanced degree.
Hands-on experience programming in Java or Go.
Experience with technical processes such as code reviews and testing.
Experience debugging issues with production systems and with monitoring and event management platforms.

sections.heading: Preferred Qualifications
bullets: Knowledge of cloud services (e.g. AWS and its common services).

Knowledge of relational databases (e.g. MySQL).
Knowledge of incident management processes (e.g. ITIL).
Knowledge of Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) principles and best practices.
Experience deploying and operating services in a live environment.

Open paid time off.
Retirement benefits with company matching.
Life insurance, parental leave, plus short-term and long-term disability.

sections.heading: About the Role
sections.paragraph: GM is hiring a technically opinionated Staff Engineer to serve as a founding member of the Hybrid Services & Reliability (HSR) team. AV Core Infrastructure (ACI) is the core engineering organization responsible for the unified, secure hybrid platform that enables all autonomous vehicle hosting, validation, and testing. You will be responsible for the measurable reliability of the "bench cloud" -- the end-to-end environment that flows from physical test benches through the secure cloud network into production runtimes. This role is on the critical path for Super Cruise, where the readiness of hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) systems is a Tier-0 requirement for safety validation.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Reliability Architecture: Lead the definition and implementation of Service Level Objectives (SLOs) and SLIs for critical hybrid cloud services, establishing ready-state verification processes.
Foundational Utilities: Own and automate core on-prem utilities, including DHCP, PXE, and NTP, to enable seamless server auto-provisioning and maintain a healthy fleet of remote CI-based HIL benches.
Observability & MTTR: Develop and maintain dashboards and alerting to identify slot availability and system degradation, driving down Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) from high-severity incidents.
Environment Integrity: Ensure uninterrupted data paths and dependency stability as data moves from ingestion at the test bench into development and staging runtimes.
Technical Mentorship: Act as a resource for colleagues with less experience, identifying opportunities to improve internal processes and services to support functional strategies.

sections.heading: Required Qualifications
bullets: Extensive professional experience in Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) with a focus on hybrid cloud platforms; coding using Python/Go.
Recognized expertise in Linux systems administration and the automation of foundational networking utilities (DHCP/PXE).
Deep experience with configuration management tools (e.g., Chef, Ansible) and Infrastructure as Code to reduce operational toil.
Proven ability to work independently on complex issues that require conceptual thinking and the wide application of principles and concepts.

sections.heading: Preferred Qualifications
bullets: Experience with Kubernetes (k8s) and managing hybrid service connectivity between on-prem and multi-cloud environments.

sections.heading: Compensation & Benefits
sections.paragraph: The salary range for this role is $184,120 to $275,450 (compensation may not be representative for positions located outside of New York, Colorado, California, or Washington); actual base salary will vary based on factors relevant to the position. An incentive pay program offers bonus payouts based on company performance, job level, and individual performance. Benefit options include medical, dental, vision, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Accounts, retirement savings plan, sickness and accident benefits, life insurance, paid vacation & holidays, tuition assistance programs, employee assistance program, and GM vehicle discounts. This role may be eligible for relocation benefits. This role is hybrid, based in Sunnyvale, with a minimum of three days a week on-site.

sre-production-engineer.title: Staff Software Engineer - SRE, GitHub & CI/CD Infrastructure

sections.paragraph: Zoox is hiring a Staff Site Reliability Engineer to own source control at Zoox, serving as the technical lead for the platform that every engineer depends on daily. This is a hands-on lead role: you'll set the direction for Zoox's Git-based monorepo, drive a potential migration to GitHub Cloud, build the tooling that smooths developer workflows, and partner with platform, security, and product teams to make source control a strategic asset rather than a bottleneck. sections.heading: What You'll Do

Plan and execute a full migration from GitHub Enterprise to GitHub Cloud: scoping, design, implementation, cutover, and validation, in partnership with security, platform, and engineering teams.

Build and operate monorepo scalability improvements across repo structure, branch strategy, code ownership, large-file handling, and CI integration, including the scripts, hooks, and automation that make them stick.
Develop the guardrails and developer-facing tooling that reduce toil for SRE and product engineering, measured by build times, merge friction, and interrupt volume.
Set the technical bar through code reviews, design proposals, and pairing, serving as the hands-on authority for source control decisions across Zoox engineering.

sections.heading: Qualifications

Strong CI/CD integration background (Buildkite, GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or GitLab CI) across code review workflows, build triggers, branch policies, and merge automation.
Experience with infrastructure as code (Terraform, Pulumi, or equivalent), major cloud platforms, and executing platform migrations against active codebases.
Strong technical leadership and written communication; able to drive alignment across infrastructure, security, and product engineering.

sections.heading: Bonus Qualifications

Familiarity with monorepo build tooling (Bazel, Buck, or similar) and supply-chain hardening practices (signed commits, dependency governance, secret scanning).
Experience with code review tooling layered on top of GitHub (Reviewable, Gerrit, or similar).

Morgan, CapitalG, and Lead Edge Capital.

This is a deeply hands-on role: you will experiment with Kotlin in IntelliJ-based IDEs every day, maintain reference projects that double as regression canaries and demo showcases, build prototypes with AI coding agents, and analyze user feedback and talk to users. You won't manage from slides — you'll manage from the editor.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Own the product vision for the Kotlin plugin: fast, correct, and trustworthy IDE support that evolves with the language
Use IntelliJ IDEA with the Kotlin plugin daily — dogfooding features, catching regressions, and validating the developer experience first-hand
Maintain and develop reference Kotlin projects that serve as showcases for new features, material for conference demos, and a living test bed for DX quality
Build prototypes and proofs-of-concept using AI coding agents to explore and validate new capabilities
Own and continuously adapt the plugin roadmap across short-term release goals, mid-term initiatives, and longer-term Kotlin ecosystem projects
Align plugin planning with Kotlin compiler releases, IntelliJ IDEA release trains, and Android Studio constraints
Make explicit trade-offs and say "no" when cost, risk, or maintenance burden outweighs user value
Define problem statements, success criteria, and scope for features — then collaborate with engineers to validate and refine

Represent core Kotlin personas — JVM backend developers, library authors, Android and Multiplatform teams — and prioritize recurring pain points: false positives, slow analysis, unreliable refactorings, onboarding pain points, AI agent integrations
Run a consistent pipeline of monthly user interviews to ground decisions in real workflows
Coordinate with the Kotlin language team, Developer Advocates, framework partners, Marketing, and other JetBrains teams
Create external visibility through what's-new posts, Kotlin User Groups meetup talks, and feature previews at events like KotlinConf, Spring I/O, Devoxx and others

sections.heading: Requirements
bullets: Professional software development experience in Java or Kotlin (this is a must)
5+ years of experience in technical product management or a comparable role in a complex technical domain
A hands-on product person who lives in the product, not someone who operates through reports and meetings alone
Experience with AI coding tools and agents, and curiosity about how they reshape developer workflows
Can define clear roadmaps with problem statements, success criteria, and explicit trade-offs
Strong communication skills in English — written and spoken — for both technical peers and executive audiences
Ready to represent the product externally at conferences, in blog posts, and in community interactions
Takes developer experience personally — not as an abstract metric, but as something felt in their own daily workflow

sections.heading: Nice to Have

Have built or contributed to evaluation pipelines, benchmarks, or quality measurement systems
Have given technical talks or published content aimed at developer audiences

technical-product-manager.title: Technical Product Manager - Compliance, AML and Sanctions

sections.heading: About Visa
sections.paragraph: Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories. At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world.

sections.heading: About the Role
sections.paragraph: The candidate selected will join the Compliance Technology Operations team within the Global Ethics & Compliance Organization, which coordinates technology development, compliance requirements, and product enablement. This role is focused on improving existing Compliance applications and third-party integrations, while exploring new experiences, enabling automation, developing a long-term vision, and partnering cross-functionally. As a technical product manager, you will serve as a liaison among technology, product, and business groups, developing features for existing and new platforms, leading integrations with new businesses, and ensuring AML & Sanctions requirements meet regulatory obligations while supporting business enablement. Technical competence in database queries, supporting large-scale platforms, troubleshooting incidents, and conducting data analysis is necessary. This is a hybrid position; expectation of days in office will be confirmed by the hiring manager.

sections.heading: Essential Skills
bullets: Define and drive product vision, strategy, and roadmap for AML, Sanctions, Fraud, Transaction Monitoring, Screening (including sanctions/watchlist screening and adverse media), KYC, Client Due Diligence (CDD), and Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) platforms, aligning compliance technology with business and regulatory priorities

Lead transformation of Fin Crime systems by enhancing core compliance applications, strengthening third-party integrations, and embedding automation across transaction monitoring, sanctions screening, adverse media analysis, alert generation, case management, and investigation workflows

Drive cross-functional execution to deliver scalable, future-ready compliance solutions that optimize alert dispositioning, strengthen risk detection, ensure regulatory adherence, and improve end-to-end AML lifecycle efficiency
Demonstrated ability to align and work towards a clear product vision and long-term roadmap that aligns with business goals
Proven experience supporting the development and articulation of strategic plans with leaders for new product development initiatives, improvement, and innovation at scale
Skilled at collaborating across multiple teams including engineering, operations, compliance, product, analytics, and business units to deliver value, including in a highly matrixed environment
Guide multi-functional teams through strategic and tactical decisions, recognizing dependencies, risks, and solutions

Well-versed in Agile methodologies (Scrum, Kanban, Lean, SAFe, Design Thinking), with hands-on experience managing backlogs (user stories, epics, features), facilitating sprint planning, and utilizing tools such as JIRA and JIRA Align

Strong technical background, including experience with planning API/service design and integration, SQL, and data querying (SQL Developer, Postgres SQL), and management of large-scale, data-driven platforms
Ability to engage in technical solutioning and brainstorming discussions with architects, engineers, and QA teams, ensuring robust and scalable solutions
