sections.heading: About ClassDojo
sections.paragraph: ClassDojo's goal is to give every child on Earth an education they love. It started by building a communication network that connects teachers, children, and families; today ClassDojo is used in the vast majority of U.S. schools and reaches tens of millions of children and families globally, and is now expanding beyond communication into building the best possible learning experiences for kids everywhere. It's a small, high-talent-density team with experience from leading technology companies that values ownership, speed, and thoughtful decision-making.

sections.heading: About the Role
sections.paragraph: We're hiring a Technical Product Manager for our Platform Team to help shape and scale the foundational systems that power all ClassDojo products. As the company expands into domain-specific platform areas (e.g., Accounts, Payments), this role will define product direction, drive prioritization, and ensure platform investments are clearly connected to user experience and business impact. This is a high-impact individual contributor role working closely with Engineering, Data, and Product teams to turn complex, ambiguous infrastructure challenges into clear, valuable product outcomes. ClassDojo is a distributed company and hires teammates who can collaborate effectively across time zones with meaningful overlap with the Americas.

sections.heading: What You'll Do
bullets: Define and drive platform product strategy: own the roadmap for key platform domains (e.g., Accounts or Payments), driving initiatives such as "Sign in with Dojo"; translate ambiguous technical challenges into clear product opportunities with defined outcomes and success metrics; ensure platform work is grounded in real user and business value

Deliver high-impact platform capabilities: break down complex systems into incremental, shippable improvements that deliver value early and often; partner closely with engineering to drive execution from concept through launch and adoption; balance tradeoffs across technical complexity, product value, and cost

Establish strong platform product practices: define how platform teams gather requirements, prioritize work, and align with stakeholders; introduce structured, data-informed decision-making into platform roadmaps; help reduce reactive work by creating clarity and alignment across teams

Partner cross-functionally: work closely with Data Platform to deliver high-impact data capabilities; collaborate with product teams to ensure platform capabilities meet real needs and are widely adopted; act as a bridge between technical systems and end-user experience

sections.heading: Requirements
bullets: 6+ years of experience as a technical PM (or equivalent between software engineer and technical PM roles)
Significant experience working on platform or infrastructure products used by multiple teams
Have operated in high-growth or scaling environments with increasing system complexity
Strong ownership and a bias for action, driving initiatives from problem definition through delivery
Can translate technical systems into clear product direction and user/business value
Comfortable working in ambiguity and turning unclear problems into structured plans
Experience working with sensitive or regulated data (e.g., identity/auth, financial, or student data)
Excellent communicator, especially in writing, and can explain complex ideas clearly

sections.heading: Nice to Have
bullets: Experience with identity, authentication, payments, or data platforms
Background spanning both technical systems and user-facing products
Experience building consumer-facing or product-led platforms

sections.heading: Compensation
sections.paragraph: Compensation is determined based on experience, location, and skillset, in alignment with ClassDojo's compensation philosophy. Salary ranges (United States): CA, WA, NY, NJ, CT states: $160,000 - $215,000 (USD). All other states in the US: $136,000 - $183,000 (USD).

sections.heading: About 1X
sections.paragraph: 1X is building humanoid robots that work in the home — doing the chores, handling the tasks, and giving people their time back. NEO is 1X's flagship, a home robot designed to move, learn, and operate in the real world alongside real people; the company is shipping it, not demoing it. Founded in 2014, 1X is now scaling and hiring with intention to build something that will genuinely change how humans spend their time.

sections.heading: About the Team
sections.paragraph: The World Model Lab builds the learned models of the physical world that let NEO understand its surroundings and act in real homes. Every model trained is capped by the quality of the data behind it — the demonstrations collected, the annotations applied, and the real-world evaluations used to measure progress. This team turns 1X's data into intelligence, and this role owns the foundation it all stands on.

sections.heading: Your Charter
sections.paragraph: The mission of this role is to own 1X's data strategy for the World Model Lab — making sure the data flowing into world models, the annotations applied to it, and the real-world evaluations that measure progress set the bar in robotics. You are the single owner accountable for input quality, which determines how fast NEO learns to operate safely in homes. This is a builder's role for a high-execution Lead PM who is ready to grow into owning strategy end to end.

sections.heading: Key Outcomes
bullets: Raise the quality of data coming in: drive measurable improvements in the quality and diversity of data feeding world model training across teleoperation, fleet (NEO), and simulation sources
Raise the quality of annotations: define the annotation quality bar and build the labeling processes and metrics that make every dataset trustworthy for training
Raise the quality of real-world evaluations: own the strategy and pipelines for real-world evals so model performance is measured accurately where it matters — in the home
Own data acquisition and sourcing: set the roadmap for where data comes from and prioritize collection to close the gaps that most limit model performance
Set the data roadmap with research: translate world model researchers' needs into a clear, prioritized data plan and drive it to delivery

sections.heading: Key Competencies
bullets: Drive execution relentlessly, turning ambiguous research needs into shipped pipelines, processes, and tooling
Define and operate quality metrics across data, annotation, and evaluation
Partner deeply with ML researchers and engineers, translating model needs into concrete data requirements
Reason about ML data, understanding how data quality and distribution shape model performance

sections.heading: Minimum Requirements
bullets: 3-5 years of product management experience, including leading technical or data products end to end
Proven track record of shipping data, ML, or infrastructure products in a fast-paced environment
Demonstrated ability to define metrics and drive measurable quality improvements
Experience working directly with ML, research, or engineering teams
Bachelor's degree in a technical field or equivalent practical experience (ideal but not a deal breaker)

sections.heading: Preferred Skills
bullets: Exposure to robotics, world models, or foundation model training
Familiarity with teleoperation, simulation, or large-scale data collection
Experience with annotation or labeling operations and data benchmarking
Appetite to grow from high-execution delivery into owning data strategy end to end

sections.heading: Culture Fit
sections.paragraph: Team members at 1X who thrive here are builders. They move fast, own their work completely, and treat time like it's the one thing you can't get back — because it is. They say what they mean, finish what they start, and hold themselves to a standard before anyone has to ask.

sections.heading: Compensation & Benefits
bullets: Compensation range: $250,000 - $300,000
Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
Generous paid time off, company holidays, and parental leave
401(k) plan with company match (100% on the first 3% of contributions, 50% on the next 2%)
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) and Health Savings Accounts (HSA) options
Commuter benefits (transit and parking)

sections.heading: About Torq
sections.paragraph: Skeletons, lasers, tattoo buses — the Torq brand grabs attention like nothing else in cybersecurity. Torq is growing fast, backed by Series D funding, 200% employee growth, and 300% revenue growth, fueled by its AI SOC platform. The team and culture have made Torq one of Forbes' Best Startup Employers in America, and a Business Insider 'startup to bet your career on.' Life at Torq is all gas, no brakes — a team of relentless, collaborative go-getters pushing the boundaries of what's possible for security automation, as the AI-native autonomous SecOps platform of choice for security teams across the Fortune 500.

sections.heading: About the Role
sections.paragraph: We are looking for an experienced Senior Product Manager to own Torq's Runtime domain — the core engine that powers millions of workflow executions for security teams worldwide. We seek an experienced, curious, self-motivated product manager to lead our events pipeline, orchestration, and execution infrastructure.

sections.heading: What You'll Be Doing
bullets: Become an expert in all runtime aspects - events pipeline, orchestration, execution, Runners, and the infrastructure that powers them
Own the end-to-end Runner experience - deployment models, resource management, connectivity, and troubleshooting
Perform customer research and technical deep-dives to understand scale requirements, reliability needs, and pain points
Analyze customer escalations to identify patterns and translate them into actionable engineering requirements
Make critical decisions with respect to architectural trade-offs, reliability, performance, and quality to meet business objectives
Work closely with engineering to define, develop, and launch new runtime capabilities across the distributed execution layer

sections.heading: The Ideal Candidate
bullets: 3-5 years of experience as a product manager on backend/infrastructure products, OR 2+ years in PM following a backend engineering role
Hands-on experience with Kubernetes (EKS, GKE, or OpenShift) and container orchestration
Cloud infrastructure fluency - comfortable with AWS and GCP compute, networking, and storage
Experience with high-scale systems handling significant event throughput or distributed execution
Data-driven, with the ability to ask the right questions
Excellent interpersonal skills, able to drive teams & interfaces without direct authority
Strong technical communication - can explain distributed systems to customers and translate their pain into specs
Computer Science degree or equivalent technical background

sections.heading: Nice to Have
bullets: Experience with message-driven architectures (PubSub, Kafka)
Familiarity with service-to-service communication
Background in security, DevOps, or automation platforms
Experience with hybrid deployments - cloud services connecting to customer-managed agents/runners

sections.heading: Application Note
sections.paragraph: Torq builds AI for a living and encourages candidates to use it to prep, research, and sharpen their best work — tighten your resume, prep for interviews, research Torq, and outline ideas for written responses. But they're hiring humans, not chatbots: show up as yourself for live interviews and final assessments, where the voice, logic, and reasoning need to be yours.
